title: About pandyaHomeLab
tagline: A live, end-to-end AI engineering platform — built on a Synology NAS, fronted by Cloudflare Tunnel. Every demo is production-style: not a notebook, not a screenshot.

sections.title: The Mission
body: pandyaHomeLab is a personal AI engineering platform that pairs **learning** with **building**. Every demo is a working production-style service — proper architecture, schema validation, container networking, experiment tracking, observability.

The platform is intentionally **domain-organised**: ML, DL, NLP, and Agentic AI each get their own Docker network, URL prefix, and project set — all following the same disciplined 4-layer pattern (per ADR-013).

sections.title: The Journey — Business Intelligence → Artificial Intelligence
body: After **20+ years in Business Intelligence**, I'm transitioning into **Artificial Intelligence** — not just a new domain, but a new way to *build*.
For most of my career, building meant typing every line. That craft taught me how things actually work, and that foundation still matters.

But I'm building differently now. I'm **pair-programming with an AI agent (Claude Code)** — describing intent, reviewing what's generated, asking *why* each choice was made, course-correcting when needed. The architecture is mine. The judgement is mine. The taste is mine. The typing — mostly, isn't.
*The shift in one line: from **writing code** to **generating efficient code, with intent**.*

sections.title: Platform Architecture
body: Four AI domains sit on isolated Docker bridge networks, each fronted by a shared Nginx reverse proxy. Each domain has its own shared infrastructure (PostgreSQL, MinIO, Redis, MLflow) so domains never cross-contaminate. Every project inside a domain follows the same 4-layer internal structure.

sections.title: Network Topology
body: Per **ADR-016**, each AI domain gets its own dedicated /24 subnet — letting services within a domain talk freely while keeping clean boundaries between domains. Nginx is the only crossing point from the proxy network into any domain network.

graph LR User([🌍 Internet]) --> CF[Cloudflare Tunnel] CF --> NX[Nginx
172.24.0.2] subgraph PROXY[pandya-proxy-network · 172.24.0.0/24] NX end subgraph ML[ml-network · 172.20.0.0/24] IRIS[ml-iris-knn
172.20.0.10] HOUS[ml-housing-linear
172.20.0.11] TIT[ml-titanic-automl
172.20.0.12] MF[ml-mlflow
172.20.0.5] PG[ml-postgres

sections.title: The 4-Layer Project Pattern
body: Every project on pandyaHomeLab — regardless of domain — follows the same per-project 4-layer architecture (locked by ADR-013). Read iris-knn once and you can navigate any other demo immediately.

bullets.label: presentation-logic
bullets.text: FastAPI routes, Pydantic v2 schemas, error handlers, demo UI.

bullets.label: application-logic
bullets.text: Workflow orchestration (the *what should happen, in what order*) + the model itself.

bullets.label: db-logic
bullets.text: Data loading, train/test splits, preprocessing, persistence.

bullets.label: shared
bullets.text: Cross-cutting utilities: config, structured logger, exceptions, constants.

links.label: Read the full ML Project Walkthrough (~1,200 lines, iris-knn as the worked example)

sections.title: Live Demos
body: All three ML demos are live. Click any to try it, or open the **About this project** drawer inside each demo for a deep dive on architecture, metrics, and code patterns.

links.label: 🌸 Iris KNN Classifier — multi-class classification, balanced data
🏠 Housing Linear Regression — continuous prediction, 20,640 census blocks
🚢 Titanic AutoML — binary classification on imbalanced data, PyCaret AutoML
📈 MLflow — experiment tracking for every run

sections.title: Roadmap
body: Phase 1 (ML) is complete. The platform now grows along two dimensions: **more domains** (DL, NLP, Agentic) and **production maturity** (CI/CD, AWS deployment, observability).

bullets.label: Phase 2 — Deep Learning
bullets.text: CNN on MNIST handwritten digits → image classification → object detection. PyTorch + interactive draw-a-digit UI.

bullets.label: Phase 3 — Natural Language Processing
bullets.text: Sentiment analysis, Named Entity Recognition, transformer-based models from HuggingFace.

bullets.label: Phase 4 — Agentic AI
bullets.text: Claude API in production. Multi-step reasoning, tool use, structured outputs.

bullets.label: Stage 3 — CI/CD
bullets.text: GitHub Actions pipelines, automated test gates, container image registry.

bullets.label: Stage 4 — AWS deployment
bullets.text: Migrate from NAS to ECS with RDS PostgreSQL, S3 artifact storage, ElastiCache Redis.

bullets.label: Stage 5 — TLS + Observability
bullets.text: Let's Encrypt certificates, Prometheus + Grafana, distributed tracing.

sections.title: About Archit
body: **Archit Pandya** — a seasoned technologist with **20+ years across enterprise BI, data engineering, and analytics**. Most recently **Manager Projects at Cognizant** (2021–2023), preceded by a **decade as BI Analytics Supervisor at Becton Dickinson** (2010–2020) and **12 years as BI Analytics Supervisor at C.R. Bard** (1998–2010) — delivering data-driven solutions for Finance, Supply Chain, Sales, and Marketing functions across Fortune-500 medical-device organisations.
**Education:** M.B.A. Finance (Fairleigh Dickinson University, 2005) · M.S. Computer Science (NJIT, 1994). **Certifications:** AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner; MicroStrategy CRD, CPA, CDP.

After decades of building enterprise BI solutions, Archit is now extending his career into **Advanced Analytics and Machine Learning** — currently through Analytics Vidhya's mentorship program, applying stacked regression ensembles to retail sales forecasting and benchmarked regression models to used-car pricing.

pandyaHomeLab is the work and the proof of the work: every demo here was built by pairing two decades of *"how things actually ship"* engineering judgement with the velocity of an AI coding agent. The platform's **depth** reflects experience; the **pace** reflects what becomes possible when a senior engineer pair-programs with a capable AI.

bullets.label: 📺 Acknowledgements
bullets.text: Dedicated in part to **CampusX** ([@campusx-official](https://www.youtube.com/@campusx-official)) and **Krish Naik** ([@krishnaik06](https://www.youtube.com/@krishnaik06)) — relentless inspiration for going beyond just *training a model* into *engineering a production-quality solution*. "More than a model."
